title: Implement azq agenda build as a thin deliverable-to-task writer
goal: Make azq/agenda/build.py validate one exact canonical deliverable and create the first narrow file-backed Agenda task records on top of the Wave A storage helpers without turning Agenda into an execution engine.
context: Stage 3 Wave A already established canonical Agenda task, DAG, and log storage helpers under azq/agenda/.
The live repository uses router-based command families and thin command modules, so Wave B should mirror the healthy Stage 2 pattern rather than reopening storage work.

AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_3.md is partially stale: it still says azq/agenda/ does not exist and frames the first commands as azq task or azq dag by goal_id, while the live repo already has Agenda storage modules and this wave is intentionally scoped to the first azq agenda command loop.
This first write path should stay deliverable-centered so Agenda remains downstream of canonical Formam deliverables instead of becoming a second planning system.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/build.py.
Add a thin entrypoint such as build_agenda(deliverable_id).
Validate the parent deliverable by exact deliverable_id through the existing Agenda or Formam storage boundary.
Allocate the next stable TASK_### id through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Generate one or more deterministic stub task records for that exact deliverable using the live canonical Agenda task schema.
Write canonical task files through existing Agenda storage helpers instead of writing files ad hoc.
Keep the first build behavior minimal, inspectable, and free of task execution, agent loops, or speculative planning logic.
acceptance_criteria: A valid canonical deliverable can produce visible TASK_###.md artifact(s) under data/agenda/tasks/.
build.py delegates persistence and schema details to the existing Agenda storage layer instead of duplicating repository logic.
The first Agenda build behavior stays intentionally small, deterministic, and file-backed.
suggested_commit_message: add agenda build command module for canonical task records

title: Implement azq agenda list as a thin presentation layer
goal: Make azq/agenda/tasks.py provide a read-only listing view over canonical Agenda task storage so the first built tasks become visible from the command layer.
context: Wave B should expose read commands only after the first canonical Agenda write path exists.
The existing Stage 2 command pattern uses small presentation modules over shared storage helpers rather than embedding repository logic in the CLI.
Agenda task records are Markdown files and should remain inspectable and terminal-readable rather than hidden behind opaque command output.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/tasks.py.
Add a read-only listing function such as list_tasks().
Load canonical tasks through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Display at least task_id, deliverable_id, title, and status.
Keep the output simple, stable, and terminal-readable.
Preserve a clear no-tasks message when canonical Agenda storage is empty.
acceptance_criteria: tasks.py can present canonical Agenda tasks without mutating task state.
The listing reads through shared Agenda storage helpers rather than duplicating repository logic.
The visible output stays focused on built tasks, not execution automation or later-stage artifact behavior.

title: Implement azq agenda show with exact-id task inspection
goal: Extend azq/agenda/tasks.py so one canonical Agenda task can be inspected clearly by exact task_id without broadening into start or complete behavior.
context: Wave B is the first command-layer wave for Agenda and should prioritize useful read behavior before state-transition commands arrive in a later wave.
Exact task lookup matters because later Agenda state changes will depend on stable canonical TASK_### identifiers.
The live storage layer already validates parent deliverables on task load, so task inspection should remain a thin reader over that foundation.
requirements: Add a reader such as show_task(task_id).
Load one task by exact task_id through the existing Agenda storage helpers.
Print the canonical task fields clearly, including deliverable linkage, dependencies, description, task intent metadata, execution notes, status, and created date.
Handle missing task ids with a readable exact-id failure message.
Keep output centered on canonical task inspection rather than execution or log mutation.
acceptance_criteria: One canonical Agenda task can be inspected by exact id.
Missing tasks fail clearly without fuzzy matching.
tasks.py remains a presentation module over shared Agenda storage helpers.
suggested_commit_message: add exact task inspection for agenda show

title: Implement azq agenda dag as a thin deliverable-scoped DAG refresher
goal: Make azq/agenda/dags.py validate one exact canonical deliverable, derive its parent-goal view from existing Formam records and Agenda tasks, and write or refresh one visible canonical DAG artifact without broadening into execution flow.
context: Wave A already created canonical DAG storage helpers, but no command-layer module currently turns those helpers into an operator-visible action.

AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_3.md describes goal-level DAG artifacts, while this wave is intentionally deliverable-triggered by operator request; the narrow live-friendly approach is to resolve the exact deliverable first, then refresh the parent goal DAG from canonical Formam deliverables and Agenda tasks.
This command should stay file-backed and inspectable, preferring conservative edge derivation over smart planning.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/dags.py.
Add a command-layer entrypoint such as build_deliverable_dag(deliverable_id) or agenda_dag(deliverable_id).
Validate the parent deliverable by exact deliverable_id through the existing storage boundary.
Resolve the parent goal from that deliverable and load canonical deliverables for the exact goal through Formam storage.
Load canonical Agenda tasks descended from those deliverables and build or refresh one GOAL_<goal_id>_DAG.json artifact through existing Agenda DAG storage helpers.
Keep dependency-edge derivation conservative and inspectable, using visible task and deliverable relationships rather than speculative planning structure.
Do not introduce task start, task completion, task logs, or automation beyond building and reading artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: A deliverable with canonical parent records can refresh one visible DAG artifact under data/agenda/dags/.
dags.py remains a thin command-layer module over shared Formam and Agenda storage helpers.
The first DAG command stays narrow, deterministic, and visibly subordinate to existing deliverable and task artifacts.
suggested_commit_message: add agenda dag command module for canonical dag refresh

title: Wire Agenda commands into the CLI
goal: Expose the first usable Agenda command loop through a dedicated Agenda router and CLI module without broadening beyond the Stage 3 Wave B command set.
context: The live top-level CLI delegates to engine routers for Scintilla, Finis, and Formam, but Agenda has no router or CLI module yet.

This wave is intentionally the first command-layer wave for Agenda, so CLI wiring should stop at build, list, show, and dag rather than reaching into start, complete, or wider Stage 3 closeout behavior.
The requested command family for this manifest is azq agenda ..., which is a deliberate live-repo judgment call even though older docs still describe azq task and azq dag command names.
requirements: Create azq/agenda/cli.py and azq/agenda/router.py.
Add command help lines for azq agenda build <deliverable_id>, azq agenda list, azq agenda show <task_id>, and azq agenda dag <deliverable_id>.
Add an agenda branch in the new Agenda CLI module with clear usage messages for each supported subcommand.
Route build, list, show, and dag to the new azq/agenda command modules.
Update azq/cli.py to dispatch through the new Agenda router alongside the existing engine routers.
Keep command handling narrow and stylistically consistent with the existing router-based CLI.
Do not introduce start, complete, status, doctor, archive, or task-running behavior in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes the first usable Agenda command loop through azq agenda ... routing.
azq agenda build, azq agenda list, azq agenda show, and azq agenda dag dispatch to dedicated Agenda modules.
CLI help and usage text stay aligned with the intentionally narrow Wave B scope.
